result:
Subject: Yesterday's payments outage — what happened and what we're doing about it

Hi there,

Yesterday, our payments service was degraded for roughly four hours, and during that window some of you saw transactions fail outright. If you were one of those users, I'm sorry. Payments are the part of our product you trust us to get right every single time, and yesterday we didn't. There's no softer way to say it.

The root cause was on us: a misconfigured cache invalidation rule, which held up fine under normal load, collapsed once a traffic spike hit it. Stale and missing entries cascaded into the request path and pushed a subset of payment attempts into a failure state. Once we identified the rule, we rolled back the change, repaired the affected cache tier, and confirmed end-to-end recovery before declaring the incident resolved.

To make sure this specific failure mode doesn't reach you again, we're moving cache and invalidation changes behind canary rollouts so traffic-spike behavior gets observed on a small slice before any full deploy, and we're adding chaos testing in staging to deliberately reproduce load spikes and invalidation storms against the payments path. Broader post-incident review is underway, and anything else we learn will feed the same process.

If your account was affected, we'll be applying a goodwill credit automatically — no action needed on your end, and you'll see it reflected within the next few days. If you believe you were impacted and don't see the credit, reply to this email and we'll make it right. Thank you for your patience, and again, I'm sorry for the disruption.

— The Payments Team
